title: Error: must provide image ID and image name to use an image: invalid argument
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Podman is telling me, after creating a successful container, to provide an image ID or image name while I give entered one.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install Podman 1.3.2 on RHEL 7.7 with basic repo, extras and beta, and make a test user without root rights.

2. Login with the test user.

3. Try to run multiple containers.

**Describe the results you received:**
Second container gives error that i need to provide Image ID. 
[test@rhel77 ~]$ podman run -d nginx
25042d922c25eaf726518a87460b51d12b5ecc791b2e988349738832935c86c1
[test@rhel77 ~]$ podman run -d nginx
Error: must provide image ID and image name to use an image: invalid argument


**Describe the results you expected:**
That the second container would start.
[test@rhel77 ~]$ podman run -d nginx
25042d922c25eaf726518a87460b51d12b5ecc791b2e988349738832935c86c1
[test@rhel77 ~]$ podman run -d nginx
d12b5ecc791b2e988349738832935c86c125042d922c25eaf726518a87460b51

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
When I stop the only container active I can start one again.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version
Version:            1.3.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.8
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.8
  podman version: 1.3.2
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.8.2
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.3.2-1.git14fdcd0.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.14.0-dev, commit: 4780496c1f6949e21c807e17b57c183e9424c90a-dirty'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"rhel"'
    version: "7.7"
  MemFree: 174981120
  MemTotal: 3973541888
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-83.dev.gitf56b4cb.fc28.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 3434860544
  SwapTotal: 3435130880
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 1
  hostname: rhel77.michiel
  kernel: 3.10.0-1049.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 139h 46m 20.66s (Approximately 5.79 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 18
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 2
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
This is strange, I don't see an issue on Fedora.
If you attempt to run nginx a third time, does it continue to give you the same error?
Of course I am using a slightly newer version.
 ```
podman version
Version:            1.4.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.5
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```
Can't reproduce with 1.3.2, rootless, on F30. I'll try 7.7 later, but this one seems like it could be an environment issue.

@rhatdan I'm using podman 1.3.2, runc 1.0.0-rc8+dev and slirp4netns-0.3.0.2

[test@localhost ~]$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                           COMMAND               CREATED         STATUS             PORTS  NAMES
5cf40bbb4d91  docker.io/library/nginx:latest  nginx -g daemon o...  23 seconds ago  Up 23 seconds ago         infallible_napier
[test@localhost ~]$ podman run -d nginx
Error: must provide image ID and image name to use an image: invalid argument
[test@localhost ~]$ podman stop 5cf4
5cf40bbb4d91f7ec92a66e61bda2c98f6c98ffbf2c8e68f628daa3f84a138950
[test@localhost ~]$ podman run -d nginx
7468b471f8995ce268748902feca34c4b606e913f14b0210f044a1b018076171
[test@localhost ~]$ podman run -d nginx
Error: must provide image ID and image name to use an image: invalid argument
[test@localhost ~]$ podman run -d nginx
Error: must provide image ID and image name to use an image: invalid argument
[test@localhost ~]$ podman run -d nginx
Error: must provide image ID and image name to use an image: invalid argument

@mheon Just installed it on another system. Same error occurs.

Alright, so it's consistent, but seemingly only on RHEL7

@michielhemme To confirm, are these fresh installs of Podman, or upgrading existing installations that have run Podman before?

@mheon, the podman installation is fresh out of the repositories. It is being delivered with runc-1.0.0-59-dev and trying to start a container then results in: 

[test@localhost ~]$ podman run -d nginx
Error: container create failed: cannot specify gid= mount options for unmapped gid in rootless containers
: internal libpod error

after upgrading runc to the latest version, I get the other error. That the first container can start but not the second one.
Could this be a vfs storage issue?  That is one difference.  RHEL7 does not support fuse-overlay.
Can't replicate with VFS on F30 - still seems RHEL specific
the 7.7 kernel is missing some ioctl's we are using.  We will need to backport 4dca13e70423e657aec98999d9c6803075359517
it applies cleanly on top of v1.3.2, and it seems to solve the problem on RHEL 7.7

Is that in 1.4? We're expecting a 1.4 with 7.7, so if it's available there
we should be set

On Tue, Jun 18, 2019, 04:05 Giuseppe Scrivano <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> it applies cleanly on top of v1.3.2, and it seems to solve the problem on
> RHEL 7.7
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3345?email_source=notifications&email_token=AB3AOCF4U4SMD7KEDHLGJNTP3CJLPA5CNFSM4HYTJ2G2YY3PNVWWK3TUL52HS4DFVREXG43VMVBW63LNMVXHJKTDN5WW2ZLOORPWSZGODX5RO6A#issuecomment-502994808>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCEXVZCB6YE46IUGLPLP3CJLPANCNFSM4HYTJ2GQ>
> .
>
Closing since it is in Master and soon to be everywhere.
